svg.text: Structure determines function — dysfunction reveals normal function
Development: Neural Plate → Neural Tube (→ CNS) + Neural Crest (→ PNS, melanocytes, facial bones)
14,000 genes control brain development | Shh (ventral, motor) vs BMP/Wnt (dorsal, sensory) | Hox genes pattern hindbrain
Nuclei (cell bodies) | Tracts (axon bundles) | CNS Glia: Astrocytes, Oligodendrocytes, Microglia, Ependyma
Cell body (soma, Nissl bodies) | Dendrites (receive, spines) | Axon (conduct AP, no Nissl) | Synapse (vesicles, cleft, receptors)
Schwann cells (myelinate ONE axon) | Dorsal root ganglia (sensory) | Cranial + Spinal nerves | Nerve = axon bundle + connective tissue
More glycolipid | No basal lamina | No regeneration
ONE Schwann cell → ONE axon segment
More phospholipid | Has basal lamina + endoneurium | Can regenerate
Gaps between myelin segments | Na+ channels concentrated at nodes
Astrocytes: recycle NTs, modulate synapses, BBB, K+ balance
Microglia: CNS immune cells | Ependyma: line ventricles, produce CSF
6 functional regions (C1–Co2) | Central grey matter (butterfly shape)
Dorsal horn (sensory, laminae 1–4) | Ventral horn (motor, lamina 9)
Spinothalamic (pain/temp) — crosses in spinal cord (anterior white commissure)
8 cervical (7 vertebrae) | 12 thoracic | 5 lumbar | 5 sacral | variable coccygeal
Each spinal nerve supplies a specific dermatome (skin strip) from C1 to Co
CN 3–12 nuclei | Vital centers | Corridor for long pathways
Functional classification: SE, VE, BE, SA, VA, SS
Inferior olive (climbing fibers, timing) | Pontine nuclei (mossy fibers, massive)
External cuneate nucleus (upper limb proprioception → cerebellum)
Thalamus: sensory relay (all senses except smell)
VPL, DLG, MG, VL nuclei | Habenular nuclei → pineal
Most rostral part of neural tube (NOT "under thalamus"!)
180 distinct Brodmann areas | M1 (4), S1 (3,1,2), V1 (17), A1 (41)
Amygdala (fear, social hierarchy) | Substantia nigra (dopamine → striatum)
4 commissures: Corpus callosum (largest), Anterior, Hippocampal, Posterior
Projection fibers (internal capsule) | Association fibers | Limbic system = discredited term!
+ Astrocytic end-feet | Absent in circumventricular organs
MRI (soft tissue), CT (hemorrhage/bone), EEG, EMG, NCS, LP
Motor: tone, reflexes, Babinski, tremor type (resting vs action)
Sensory: dermatomal loss | Cognitive: aphasia, apraxia, memory
Corticospinal tract (voluntary, fine distal limb) — crosses at pyramidal decussation
Basal ganglia circuit (select movements) | Cerebellum (coordinate, real-time adjust)
α-Motor neuron = final common pathway (Sherrington) | Extrapyramidal system = discredited term
Spinothalamic (pain/temperature/crude touch) → crosses in spinal cord
Visual (retina → LGN → V1) | Auditory (cochlea → MGN → A1) | Vestibular | Olfactory (bypasses thalamus!)
Two-neuron chain: preganglionic (CNS → ganglion) + postganglionic (ganglion → target)
Sympathetic: T1–L2, paravertebral chain, short pre/long post, NA (fight or flight)
Parasympathetic: CN 3,7,9,10 + S2–S4, near-organ ganglia, long pre/short post, ACh (rest & digest)
Sleep: VLPO switch → inhibits arousal centers (LC, Raphe, Tegmental, TMN) | 90-min cycles
Memory: Semantic (facts) vs Episodic (personal) | Hippocampus = formation; Cortex = storage
Aphasia: Wernicke (receptive, BA22) vs Broca (expressive, BA44–45) | Apraxia (parietal/prefrontal)
MCA most common | Internal capsule | "Time is brain"
Substantia nigra degeneration | Resting tremor | L-DOPA | DBS
Hippocampus first | Aβ plaques (extracellular) | Tau tangles (intracellular)
Cortical hyperexcitability | EEG diagnostic | Generalized vs TLE
CST damage: ↑tone, ↑reflexes, Babinski | MN damage: ↓tone, ↓reflexes, atrophy
CSF: elevated WBC | Neck stiffness | Bacterial vs Viral
Virus → motor neurons | Flaccid paralysis | Nearly eradicated by vaccine
UMN + LMN degeneration | Fatal within ~3 years
Autosomal dominant | CAG repeat | Striatum | Chorea | Onset 35–50
4 subtypes: Spastic, Ataxic, Athetoid, Mixed | Developmental damage
Dopamine/Serotonin | Hallucinations | SSRIs, CBT | Bipolar: Li, valproate
Thiamine deficiency (alcohol) | Mammillary body atrophy | Confabulation
PrPsc misfolding | Rapid dementia | Sporadic 1:1,000,000 | vCJD from BSE
Methylphenidate | ASD: NOT vaccines! | OCD: SSRIs ~60% respond
Folate deficiency → NTD | Alcohol → FAS | Rubella → deaf/blind
Core Principle: Histological Structure → Anatomical Organization → Functional Circuitry → Clinical Signs When Disrupted
